curriculum.sourceVerificationNote: Academic lead must supply verified CP identifiers before curriculum-alignment claims.

competencies.title: Familiar spoken interaction
competencies.description: Interact in everyday school situations: introductions, small talk, preferences, invitations, clarification.

competencies.title: Everyday listening comprehension
competencies.description: Understand announcements and informal conversations for gist and detail.

competencies.title: Descriptive texts
competencies.description: Comprehend and produce descriptions of people and places.

competencies.title: Recount texts
competencies.description: Comprehend and produce recounts of past events.

competencies.title: Narrative basics
competencies.description: Tell a simple connected story with sequencing language.

competencies.title: Procedure texts
competencies.description: Explain and follow simple sequenced procedures.

competencies.title: Functional reading and writing
competencies.description: Use posters, forms, and short messages for real purposes.

competencies.title: Language awareness in context
competencies.description: Use present and past forms, prepositions of place, and question forms to support communication.

objectives.title: Introduce yourself and respond naturally in a new-school context
Open, maintain, and close small talk
Understand school announcements for gist and detail
Make, accept, and decline invitations politely
Recount past events with past simple
Ask for help, repetition, and clarification
Explain a simple procedure in sequence
Write short messages with appropriate informal register

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Introduce yourself so people want to keep talking
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Setelah pelajaran ini, Anda dapat memperkenalkan diri secara alami dan menanggapi perkenalan orang lain.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can introduce myself and respond naturally on a first meeting.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Pikirkan pertemuan pertama Anda di sekolah baru. Jawab singkat dalam bahasa Inggris.
promptsEn: What do people usually say first when they meet?
What do you want a new classmate to know about you?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Perkenalan alami memiliki tiga bagian: sapaan, informasi singkat, dan pertanyaan balik. Pertanyaan balik membuat percakapan terus berjalan.

patterns.en: Hi, I'm ___. Nice to meet you.
I'm from ___, and I'm really into ___.
patterns.noteId: "into" berarti sangat menyukai; terdengar alami untuk remaja.

patterns.en: How about you?
patterns.noteId: Pertanyaan balik singkat agar lawan bicara ikut bercerita.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Hey, are you new here? I haven't seen you before. B: Yeah, I just moved from Makassar. I'm Dinda. A: Nice to meet you, Dinda. I'm Bayu. So, how do you like it so far? B: It's big! I got lost twice this morning, honestly. A: Everyone does. What are you into? Any clubs yet? B: Not yet. I like photography, though. How about you?

tasks.promptEn: Where is Dinda from?
tasks.answerEn: Makassar
tasks.explanationId: Informasi disebutkan langsung setelah "I just moved from".

tasks.promptEn: How does Dinda keep the conversation going at the end?
tasks.answerEn: She asks "How about you?"
tasks.explanationId: Pertanyaan balik adalah pola inti pelajaran ini.

tasks.promptEn: What detail does Dinda share about her morning?
tasks.answerEn: She got lost twice.
tasks.explanationId: Detail kecil membuat perkenalan terasa hidup.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: A classmate says: "Hi, I'm Raka. Nice to meet you." Choose the most natural reply.
optionsEn: Nice to meet you too. I'm ___.
I am pleased to be acquainted with your person.
tasks.explanationId: Jawaban kedua tidak alami; jawaban ketiga terlalu formal untuk teman sebaya.

tasks.promptEn: Make this sound more natural: "I like football very much."
tasks.answerEn: I'm really into football.
tasks.explanationId: "be into" adalah ungkapan sehari-hari untuk kesukaan yang kuat.

tasks.promptEn: Which follow-up keeps the conversation alive?
tasks.explanationId: Pertanyaan balik mengundang lawan bicara untuk berbagi.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record a 30-second self-introduction for your first day: name, where you are from, one thing you are into, and one question for your new classmate.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Ungkapan pelajaran ini langsung dipakai saat bertemu Hana di gerbang sekolah pada Episode 1.

questions.promptEn: Your new classmate says "I'm into robotics." What does she mean?
optionsEn: She is inside the robotics room.

questions.promptEn: Choose the best closing for a short first chat before class starts.
questions.explanationId: "See you around" santai dan ramah; pilihan lain aneh atau kasar.

questions.promptEn: Which introduction gives the listener something to respond to?
optionsEn: I'm Sinta. I just moved here, and I'm into badminton. How about you?
My name is Sinta and that is my name.
questions.explanationId: Informasi singkat plus pertanyaan balik membuat percakapan berlanjut.

criteria.descriptorId: Jawaban menyelesaikan tuntutan end-check dan tetap relevan.
Grammar dan kosakata target digunakan secara akurat.
Makna jawaban jelas dan dapat dibuktikan dari stimulus.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Start and keep a light conversation going
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan belajar membuka obrolan ringan, meresponsnya, dan menutupnya dengan sopan.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can start, continue, and end small talk with a classmate.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab dalam bahasa Inggris.
promptsEn: What topics are safe to talk about with someone you just met?
What topics might be uncomfortable?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Obrolan ringan biasanya dimulai dari situasi bersama: cuaca, pelajaran, kantin, acara sekolah. Tanggapan yang baik menambahkan informasi, bukan hanya "yes" atau "no".

patterns.en: Crowded today, huh?
Did you catch the announcement this morning?
Anyway, I'd better get to class.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Ugh, the canteen line is so long today. B: Right? It's because of the bazaar, I think. A: Oh yeah, that's this week, isn't it? Are you going? B: Probably. My friend has a stall there. A: Nice. Anyway, I'd better run, class starts in five. B: See you!

tasks.promptEn: What shared situation starts this small talk?
tasks.answerEn: The long canteen line.
tasks.explanationId: Komentar tentang situasi bersama adalah pembuka paling mudah.

tasks.promptEn: How does speaker A end the chat politely?
tasks.answerEn: "Anyway, I'd better run, class starts in five."
tasks.explanationId: "Anyway" + alasan singkat adalah penutup yang halus.

tasks.promptEn: Does B answer with only yes/no?
tasks.answerEn: No, B adds information about the bazaar and a friend's stall.
tasks.explanationId: Menambahkan informasi menjaga percakapan tetap hidup.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: It starts raining while you wait with a classmate. Choose the most natural opener.
optionsEn: Wow, it's really coming down, huh?
You. Talk to me about rain.
tasks.explanationId: Pilihan kedua terlalu formal; pilihan ketiga terlalu memerintah.

tasks.promptEn: Classmate: "Did you watch the match last night?" Which reply keeps the talk going?
optionsEn: Yeah, that last goal was crazy! Did you stay up for it?
tasks.explanationId: Tanggapan + detail + pertanyaan balik = pola emas obrolan ringan.

tasks.promptEn: Close this chat politely: you need to go to the library.
tasks.answerEn: Anyway, I'd better head to the library. See you!
tasks.explanationId: Gunakan "anyway" dan alasan singkat, lalu salam penutup.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: You are in the canteen line behind a classmate you barely know. Record a short small-talk exchange: open with the situation, respond to their answer, and close politely (both roles, 40 seconds).

lessonBlocks.noteId: Dipraktikkan bersama Hana pada Episode 1 dan di kantin pada Episode 3.

questions.promptEn: Which is the safest small-talk topic with a new classmate?
questions.explanationId: Situasi bersama aman; topik pribadi sensitif dihindari.

questions.promptEn: "Did you catch the announcement?" Here, "catch" means…
What makes "Anyway, I'd better get going" polite?
optionsEn: It signals closing softly and gives a reason.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Catch what matters in school announcements
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan berlatih menangkap inti dan detail penting dari pengumuman sekolah berbahasa Inggris.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can understand the topic, time, place, and required action of a school announcement.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: What information do announcements usually contain?
What happens if you miss the time or place?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Pengumuman selalu menjawab: apa, siapa, kapan, di mana, dan apa yang harus dilakukan. Dengarkan kata sinyal seperti "attention", "please note", "don't forget".

patterns.en: Attention, all Grade 10 students…
…will be held in the assembly hall at 10 a.m.
patterns.noteId: "be held" = diadakan; catat tempat dan waktu.

patterns.en: Please sign up at the student affairs office by Friday.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: Attention, all students. This year's club fair will be held in the assembly hall this Thursday, starting at ten a.m. Classes will end early at nine thirty. Every club will have a booth, so bring your questions. If you want to start a new club, please collect a proposal form from the student affairs office by Wednesday afternoon. Don't forget: booths close at one p.m. sharp.

tasks.promptEn: What event is announced?
tasks.answerEn: The club fair.
tasks.explanationId: Inti pengumuman disebut di kalimat kedua.

tasks.promptEn: Where and when will it be held?
tasks.answerEn: In the assembly hall, Thursday at 10 a.m.
tasks.explanationId: Tempat dan waktu selalu berdampingan dengan "be held".

tasks.promptEn: What must students do to start a new club?
tasks.answerEn: Collect a proposal form from the student affairs office by Wednesday afternoon.

tasks.promptEn: What does "at one p.m. sharp" mean?
tasks.answerEn: Exactly at 1 p.m., not later.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: "Submissions are due by Monday." You can submit…
"The meeting has been moved up to 8 a.m." The meeting is now…
Which word signals who the announcement is for?

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Listen to (read) the announcement again and write a two-line reminder note for a friend who missed it: event, place, time, and one deadline.
supportEn: Club fair — Thursday, 10 a.m., assembly hall.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Keterampilan ini menentukan apakah Anda menemukan stan Bridge Club di Episode 4.

questions.promptEn: "The tryouts will be held on the main field." "Held" means…
An announcement says "booths close at 1 p.m. sharp." You arrive at 1:10. What happens?
The most important first thing to catch in any announcement is…
optionsEn: who it is for and what it is about
questions.explanationId: Sasaran dan topik menentukan apakah Anda perlu detailnya.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Describe people and places so others can picture them
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menyusun deskripsi orang dan tempat dengan urutan yang jelas: kesan umum, lalu detail.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can describe a person or place with a clear general impression and supporting details.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: Describe your classroom in three English words.
Think of a friend: what is the first thing people notice about them?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Deskripsi yang baik bergerak dari umum ke khusus. Untuk orang: penampilan lalu kepribadian. Untuk tempat: kesan umum lalu bagian-bagiannya. Urutan kata sifat: opini sebelum fakta (a cozy small room).

patterns.en: She's easy to spot — she always wears a red hijab.
patterns.noteId: "easy to spot" = mudah dikenali; detail khas membantu pendengar.

patterns.en: The library is quiet and a little old, but really comfortable.
It's the tall building right next to the canteen.
patterns.noteId: Posisi relatif membuat tempat mudah ditemukan.

lessonBlocks.textEn: Cakrawala's media workshop is the busiest room on campus. From outside it looks like an ordinary classroom, but inside it is full of screens, cables, and half-finished projects. Posters from old student films cover one wall. The room smells faintly of coffee because the seniors practically live there. In the corner sits Ryo's desk — the tidiest spot in the whole messy room, with tools lined up like a hospital tray.

tasks.promptEn: What is the general impression of the workshop?
tasks.answerEn: It is the busiest room on campus.

tasks.promptEn: Which detail contrasts with the messy room?
tasks.answerEn: Ryo's desk is the tidiest spot, with tools lined up neatly.
tasks.explanationId: Kontras membuat deskripsi hidup dan menggambarkan karakter.

tasks.promptEn: Why does the room smell of coffee?
tasks.answerEn: Because the seniors spend very long hours there.

tasks.promptEn: Choose the natural adjective order.
Combine into one description: "The canteen is loud. The canteen is friendly."
tasks.answerEn: The canteen is loud but friendly.

tasks.promptEn: Which sentence helps someone find a place?
optionsEn: It's right next to the canteen, across from the library.
It is a place with walls.
tasks.explanationId: Posisi relatif (next to, across from) memandu pendengar.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Write a 60–80 word description of one place in your school for a visitor: general impression first, then two details, then where to find it.
supportEn: From outside it looks…, but inside…

lessonBlocks.noteId: Anda akan mendeskripsikan Bridge Club kepada calon anggota di Episode 6 dan kampus kepada Maya di Episode 8.

questions.promptEn: "She's easy to spot" means…
A good description usually starts with…
questions.explanationId: Umum ke khusus memudahkan pembaca membayangkan.

questions.promptEn: "The lab is right across from the library." Where is the lab?
optionsEn: Facing the library on the other side

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Ask for and give directions people can actually follow
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menggunakan preposisi tempat dan urutan langkah untuk memberi arah, serta bertanya arah dengan sopan.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can ask for directions politely and give directions in clear steps.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: When did you last get lost? What did you do?
Which is easier: asking for directions or reading a map?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Arah yang jelas memakai urutan (first, then), belokan (turn left/right), jarak (go past, go straight), dan posisi (next to, behind, on your left). Bertanya arah dimulai dengan "Excuse me".

patterns.en: Excuse me, how do I get to the library?
Go straight past the canteen, then turn left.
It's on your right, just behind the main hall.
patterns.noteId: Akhiri dengan posisi akhir yang jelas.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Excuse me, how do I get to the media workshop? B: Sure. Go straight down this corridor, past the science labs. Turn right at the stairs — don't go up, just turn. It's the second door on your left, right across from the printing room. A: Past the labs, right at the stairs, second door on the left. Got it. Thanks! B: No problem. You can't miss it, the door is covered in film posters.

tasks.promptEn: Where should A turn right?
tasks.answerEn: At the stairs.
tasks.explanationId: Titik belok selalu diikat pada penanda (landmark).

tasks.promptEn: How does A confirm the directions?
tasks.answerEn: A repeats the key steps back.
tasks.explanationId: Mengulang langkah adalah strategi konfirmasi penting.

tasks.promptEn: What makes the workshop easy to find at the end?
tasks.answerEn: The door is covered in film posters.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: Complete: "Go ___ the canteen and turn left."
Make this polite: "Where is the teachers' office?"
tasks.answerEn: Excuse me, could you tell me where the teachers' office is?
tasks.explanationId: "Excuse me" + pertanyaan tidak langsung terdengar sopan.

tasks.promptEn: "It's the second door on your left." Which door is it?
optionsEn: The second door on the left side
tasks.explanationId: Urutan + sisi menentukan pintu yang tepat.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record directions from your classroom to the canteen (or another real place) in 3–4 steps. Use one landmark, one turn, and a clear final position.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Dipakai saat tersesat mencari kelas 10-B di Episode 2, dan menjadi kunci tur kampus untuk Maya di Episode 8.

questions.promptEn: "Go past the library" means…
optionsEn: walk beyond the library without entering

questions.promptEn: Which is the best final step of directions?
optionsEn: "It's on your right, across from the lab."

questions.promptEn: Someone gives you long directions. The best response is…
optionsEn: repeat the key steps to confirm
questions.explanationId: Konfirmasi mencegah tersesat dan melatih menyimak.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Talk about routines and plan schedules together
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan memakai present simple dan kata keterangan frekuensi untuk membicarakan rutinitas dan menyusun jadwal.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can describe my routine and negotiate a shared schedule.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: What time does your school day usually start?
What do you always do after school?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Rutinitas memakai present simple. Kata frekuensi (always, usually, sometimes, never) berdiri sebelum kata kerja utama tetapi sesudah "be". Jadwal memakai "on Mondays", "at 3 p.m.", "every week".

patterns.en: I usually get to school before seven.
Practice is always on Wednesdays.
Does Thursday work for you?
patterns.noteId: Cara alami menawarkan jadwal — "work" berarti cocok.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: So when should the club meet? B: I have basketball on Mondays and Thursdays. A: What about Wednesdays? I'm free after three. B: Wednesday works. Not too late, though — I catch the four thirty bus. A: Three to four fifteen, then. Every week? B: Every week. I never miss the bus, just so you know.

tasks.promptEn: Why can't B meet on Mondays or Thursdays?
tasks.answerEn: B has basketball practice.
tasks.explanationId: Bentrok jadwal dinyatakan dengan present simple.

tasks.promptEn: What limits the meeting length?
tasks.answerEn: B catches the 4:30 bus.

tasks.promptEn: What does "Wednesday works" mean?
tasks.answerEn: Wednesday is suitable / fits the schedule.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: Choose the correct sentence.
Ask if Friday is suitable for your friend.
tasks.answerEn: Does Friday work for you?
tasks.explanationId: Pola "Does ___ work for you?" untuk menawarkan waktu.

tasks.promptEn: Complete: "The club meets ___ Wednesdays ___ 3 p.m."

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record yourself proposing a weekly study schedule to a friend: say your routine, suggest a day and time, and ask if it works for them (30–40 seconds).

lessonBlocks.noteId: Dipakai saat menyusun jadwal latihan Bridge Club di Episode 5 dan menyusun agenda kunjungan Maya di Episode 7.

questions.promptEn: "Does Tuesday work for you?" is asking…
optionsEn: if you have a job on Tuesday
if Tuesday is a working day

questions.promptEn: Choose the correct frequency-word position.
optionsEn: I go always home at four.
I always go home at four.
Always I go at four home.

questions.promptEn: "I catch the 4:30 bus" tells us…

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Find what you need fast in posters and forms
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan berlatih membaca cepat (scanning) teks fungsional: poster, formulir, dan pengumuman tertulis.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can scan posters and forms to find requirements, dates, and contacts.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: Do you read every word of a poster? Why not?
What information do you look for first on a sign-up form?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Teks fungsional tidak dibaca kata per kata. Cari kata kunci: who (siapa boleh ikut), when (kapan), where (di mana), how (cara daftar), dan syarat seperti "open to", "required", "no experience needed".

patterns.en: Open to all Grade 10–12 students.
patterns.noteId: "open to" menunjukkan siapa yang boleh ikut.

patterns.en: No experience needed — beginners welcome!
patterns.noteId: Frasa khas poster yang mengundang pemula.

patterns.en: Fill in this form and hand it in by Friday.

lessonBlocks.textEn: GLOBAL COMMUNICATION CLUB — RESTART! Ever wanted to speak English without feeling judged? So do we. We meet Wednesdays, 3:00–4:15, Club Room 2 (the one with the world map on the door). No experience needed — nervous speakers especially welcome. What we do: games, movie talk, guest visits, and one big project each semester. Open to all grades and all tracks, SMA and SMK. Interested? Fill in the form below and drop it in the box outside Room 2 by Friday. Questions? Find Hana in 11-B or message the club account. — Form: Name / Class / Track / Why do you want to join? (one sentence is fine) / Have you ever… spoken English outside class? (yes/no, be honest, no one checks)

tasks.promptEn: Who can join the club?
tasks.answerEn: All grades and all tracks, SMA and SMK.

tasks.promptEn: What must an interested student do by Friday?
tasks.answerEn: Fill in the form and drop it in the box outside Room 2.

tasks.promptEn: What tone does the poster use, and how do you know?
tasks.answerEn: Friendly and welcoming — e.g., "nervous speakers especially welcome", "no one checks".
tasks.explanationId: Nada teks memengaruhi siapa yang berani mendaftar.

tasks.promptEn: Where exactly does the club meet?
tasks.answerEn: Club Room 2, the one with the world map on the door.
tasks.explanationId: Detail lokasi sering diberi ciri khas.

tasks.promptEn: "Hand in the form" means…
optionsEn: hold the form in your hand

tasks.promptEn: A poster says "Auditions: members only." You are not a member. Can you audition?
The fastest way to find a deadline on a poster is to scan for…
tasks.explanationId: Scanning mencari pola informasi, bukan membaca semua.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Fill in the club form for yourself in English: name, class, track, one honest sentence about why you want to join, and your yes/no answer.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Poster inilah yang Anda temukan di pameran klub pada Episode 4.

questions.promptEn: "Beginners welcome" tells you…
"Fill in" a form means…
Which question is the form NOT asking?
questions.explanationId: Scanning juga berarti tahu apa yang TIDAK ada.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Invite people — and say no without hurting anyone
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan belajar mengundang, menerima, dan menolak undangan dengan pelembut (softener) yang menjaga hubungan.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can make, accept, and politely decline invitations.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: How do you feel when someone rejects your invitation flatly?
How do you usually say no politely in Indonesian?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Menolak undangan dalam bahasa Inggris hampir selalu memakai tiga bagian: apresiasi ("I'd love to"), alasan singkat ("but I've got practice"), dan alternatif atau harapan ("maybe next time?"). Tanpa pelembut, penolakan terdengar kasar meski tata bahasanya benar.

patterns.en: Do you want to join us for lunch?
I'd love to, but I've got homework due. Rain check?
patterns.noteId: "Rain check" = lain kali ya — sangat alami.

patterns.en: Sure, count me in!

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Hey, a few of us are checking out the new bakso place after school. Want to come? B: Ah, I'd love to, but I promised my mom I'd be home early today. C: Next time, then. What about you, Rin? D: Sure, count me in. I've been wanting to try it. A: Nice. B, we'll drag you along next week, no excuses. B: Deal. Save me a seat — and don't order everything without me.

tasks.promptEn: What three parts make B's refusal polite?
tasks.answerEn: Appreciation (I'd love to), a short reason (promised my mom), and future hope (Deal / next week).
tasks.explanationId: Pola tiga bagian adalah inti pelajaran ini.

tasks.promptEn: How does D accept?
tasks.answerEn: "Sure, count me in."

tasks.promptEn: Does the group stay friendly after the refusal? How do you know?
tasks.answerEn: Yes — they joke about dragging B along next week and B says "Save me a seat".
tasks.explanationId: Penolakan yang baik tidak merusak suasana.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: A classmate invites you to watch a match, but you are busy. Choose the best refusal.
optionsEn: I'd love to, but I've got extra class today. Next time for sure!
tasks.explanationId: Pilihan pertama dan ketiga benar tata bahasanya, tetapi lemah secara sosial.

tasks.promptEn: Invite a friend to study together tomorrow, casually.
tasks.answerEn: Want to study together tomorrow?
tasks.explanationId: "Want to…?" adalah undangan santai yang umum.

tasks.promptEn: "Rain check?" means…
optionsEn: Can we do it another time?
tasks.explanationId: Idiom untuk menunda dengan niat tetap ikut.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record two turns: (1) invite a classmate to your club's first meeting; (2) then politely decline a different invitation using the three-part pattern.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Menentukan suasana makan siang di Episode 3 dan keberhasilan merekrut anggota di Episode 6.

questions.promptEn: Which refusal keeps the friendship warm?
optionsEn: "I'd love to, but I have practice. Save me a seat next time!"
"I regret to decline your kind invitation herewith."
questions.explanationId: Pelembut + alasan + harapan; pilihan ketiga terlalu formal untuk teman.

questions.promptEn: "Count me in" means…
Why is "No." alone a weak refusal between friends?
optionsEn: It is socially cold — it gives no appreciation or reason.
questions.explanationId: Masalahnya sosial, bukan tata bahasa — inti dimensi komunikasi.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Tell what happened, in order, in the past
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menulis dan menceritakan pengalaman lampau (recount) dengan past simple dan urutan waktu.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can recount a past experience with correct past forms and clear order.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: What did you do last weekend? One sentence.
Which is harder: remembering events or putting verbs in the past?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Recount memakai past simple (went, saw, was) dan penanda urutan (last Saturday, first, after that, in the end). Struktur: orientasi (siapa, kapan, di mana) → rangkaian kejadian → komentar penutup.

patterns.en: Last Saturday, my cousins came over.
After that, we tried to cook nasi goreng — and burned it.
patterns.noteId: "After that" menjaga urutan; detail kecil menghidupkan cerita.

patterns.en: It was a mess, but honestly, it was the best part.
patterns.noteId: Komentar penutup memberi makna pada cerita.

lessonBlocks.textEn: Last Sunday, my father finally taught me to ride his old motorcycle. First, he made me push it around the yard for twenty minutes — no engine, just pushing. I thought he was joking. Then he explained the balance, and after that, he let me start the engine. I rode in a straight line for about ten meters and immediately drove into the fence. My father laughed so hard he had to sit down. In the end, I didn't really learn to ride that day, but I learned that my father laughs like a broken horn.

tasks.promptEn: What three orientation facts does the first sentence give?
tasks.answerEn: When (last Sunday), who (my father and I), what (learning to ride).

tasks.promptEn: List the sequence markers in order.
tasks.answerEn: First, Then, after that, In the end.

tasks.promptEn: What makes the ending effective?
tasks.answerEn: A personal comment/reflection with humor.
tasks.explanationId: Recount ditutup komentar, bukan sekadar kejadian terakhir.

tasks.promptEn: Put in the past: "We eat at the canteen and meet our teacher."
tasks.answerEn: We ate at the canteen and met our teacher.

tasks.promptEn: Choose the correct sentence.
Which order marker fits the LAST event?

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Write an 80–100 word recount of something real from your last weekend: orientation, three events in order, and one closing comment. Include at least one thing that went wrong.
supportEn: Last…, First…, Then…, After that…, In the end…

lessonBlocks.noteId: Keterampilan ini memuncak saat Anda menceritakan kisah satu semester di Episode 9.

questions.promptEn: Which verb is the correct past of "teach"?
A recount usually ends with…
optionsEn: a personal comment on the experience

questions.promptEn: "He laughed so hard he had to sit down" — why include details like this?
optionsEn: They make the recount vivid and personal.
questions.explanationId: Detail hidup membedakan cerita yang menarik.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Follow fast, casual talk between friends
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan berlatih memahami percakapan santai: bentuk singkat (gonna, wanna), sikap pembicara, dan makna tersirat.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can follow an informal conversation and catch the speakers' attitudes.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: Why is friends' English harder to hear than a teacher's English?
What do you do when you miss a word in a conversation?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Percakapan santai penuh bentuk pendek: gonna (going to), wanna (want to), dunno (don't know), kinda (kind of). Nada suara membawa sikap: antusias, ragu, bercanda. Anda tidak perlu setiap kata — tangkap arah dan sikap.

patterns.en: I'm gonna check out the new club.
I dunno, sounds kinda boring.
Come on, give it a chance!
patterns.noteId: "Come on" membujuk; sikap pembicara jelas dari frasa ini.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: You going to the club fair tomorrow? B: I dunno. I was gonna skip it, honestly. A: What? Come on, everyone's going. B: That's exactly why. It's gonna be packed. A: Okay, fair. But the food stalls open at ten, and I'm not queueing alone. B: …You should've started with the food. Fine, I'm in. A: Knew it. Meet you at the gate at nine forty-five. B: Make it nine fifty. I'm not that excited.

tasks.promptEn: Why does B want to skip the fair at first?
tasks.answerEn: It will be too crowded ("packed").

tasks.promptEn: What changes B's mind?
tasks.answerEn: The food stalls.
tasks.explanationId: "You should've started with the food" menunjukkan perubahan sikap.

tasks.promptEn: What does "Make it nine fifty" show about B's attitude?
tasks.answerEn: B agrees but jokingly keeps some reluctance.
tasks.explanationId: Sikap sering muncul di detail kecil, bukan pernyataan langsung.

tasks.promptEn: What does "packed" mean here?
tasks.answerEn: Very crowded.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: "I was gonna skip it" means…
"Fine, I'm in" signals…
tasks.explanationId: "I'm in" = ikut; "fine" di sini setengah menyerah, setengah setuju.

tasks.promptEn: Which strategy helps most when friends talk fast?
optionsEn: Translate every word in your head
Follow the attitude and key words, then confirm
tasks.explanationId: Menangkap arah + konfirmasi lebih realistis daripada kata per kata.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Summarize the conversation aloud in 3–4 sentences: what was decided, and how did each speaker feel? Use at least one short form (gonna/kinda).

lessonBlocks.noteId: Percakapan kantin di Episode 3 dan rapat klub di Episode 5 memakai gaya santai ini.

questions.promptEn: "kinda" is a casual form of…
"It's gonna be packed" predicts…
"You should've started with the food" implies…
optionsEn: the food argument was the most convincing
questions.explanationId: Makna tersirat: argumen makanan yang berhasil membujuk.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Say what you like — with the right strength
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menyatakan suka, tidak suka, dan preferensi dengan tingkat kekuatan yang tepat, dari "love" sampai "can't stand".
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can express likes, dislikes, and preferences at the right strength.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: Name one thing you love and one you can't stand.
Is "I don't really like it" strong or soft?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Kesukaan punya skala: love → really like → like → don't mind → not really into → can't stand. Preferensi memakai "I'd rather" atau "I prefer X to Y". Melunakkan ketidaksukaan menjaga sopan santun: "I'm not really a fan of…" lebih halus daripada "I hate…".

patterns.en: I'd rather play than watch.
I'm not really a fan of horror movies.
I don't mind waiting.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Movie night ideas — horror or comedy? B: Honestly? I can't stand horror. I don't sleep for a week. A: Noted. Comedy it is. What about the new superhero one? C: I don't mind it, but I'd rather watch something we can laugh at together. B: Same. And snacks matter more than the movie anyway. A: You two are impossible. Comedy, extra popcorn. Done.

tasks.promptEn: Rank the three speakers' feelings about horror.
tasks.answerEn: B strongly dislikes it (can't stand); A and C don't state strong feelings.

tasks.promptEn: What does C prefer, and how is it expressed?
tasks.answerEn: Something they can laugh at together — "I'd rather watch…".

tasks.promptEn: What does "I don't mind it" tell you?
tasks.answerEn: C is neutral about the superhero movie.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: Which is the softest way to refuse a food you dislike?
optionsEn: I'm not really a fan of durian, sorry!

tasks.promptEn: Express a preference: playing futsal > watching futsal.
tasks.answerEn: I'd rather play futsal than watch it.

tasks.promptEn: "I don't mind" means…

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record 30 seconds about your tastes: one thing you love, one you don't mind, one you're not really into, and one preference with "I'd rather". Make it true.
supportEn: I'm not really a fan of…

lessonBlocks.noteId: Menentukan pilihan meja dan obrolan di kantin pada Episode 3, dan mencocokkan calon anggota dengan klub di Episode 6.

questions.promptEn: Order from strongest dislike to neutral:
optionsEn: can't stand → not really into → don't mind
don't mind → can't stand → not really into
not really into → don't mind → can't stand

questions.promptEn: "I'd rather walk" implies…
Why say "I'm not really a fan of it" instead of "I hate it" to a friend's cooking?

expressions.expression: I'm not really a fan of ___

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Never stay lost: ask, check, confirm
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Ini adalah perangkat penyelamat Anda: meminta pengulangan, menanyakan makna, dan mengecek pemahaman. Perangkat ini akan terus dipakai sampai kelas 12.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can ask for repetition, ask what something means, and confirm my understanding.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab jujur.
promptsEn: What do you do when you don't understand English: guess, stay silent, or ask?
Why do many students choose silence?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Penutur yang baik bukan yang tidak pernah bingung — tetapi yang bisa keluar dari kebingungan. Tiga alat: (1) minta ulang, (2) tanya makna, (3) konfirmasi dengan kata sendiri. Bertanya bukan tanda lemah; diam adalah masalah sebenarnya.

patterns.en: Sorry, could you say that again?
What does "deadline" mean?
So you mean we meet after class, right?

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: A: Okay, so bring the consent form tomorrow, or you can't join the field trip. B: Sorry, could you say that again? The what form? A: Consent form. It means your parents sign it to give permission. B: Ah, got it. So my parents sign it, and I bring it tomorrow — right? A: Exactly. And don't fold it, the office scans them. B: One more thing — what does "scan" mean here? A: They photograph it into the computer. B: Okay. Unfolded, signed, tomorrow. Thanks!

tasks.promptEn: Which three clarification tools does B use?
tasks.answerEn: Asking for repetition, asking meaning, and confirming in his own words.
tasks.explanationId: Ketiga alat muncul dalam satu percakapan nyata.

tasks.promptEn: What happens because B asks instead of staying silent?
tasks.answerEn: B fully understands what to do (signed, unfolded, tomorrow).

tasks.promptEn: How does B confirm at the end?
tasks.answerEn: By summarizing: "Unfolded, signed, tomorrow."

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: Your teacher speaks too fast. The best response is…
optionsEn: Stay silent and copy your friend later.
"Sorry, could you slow down a little? I want to get this right."
tasks.explanationId: Pilihan ketiga benar tata bahasa tetapi terdengar menyalahkan.

tasks.promptEn: Ask the meaning of "venue".
tasks.answerEn: What does "venue" mean?
tasks.explanationId: Pola tanya makna: What does ___ mean?

tasks.promptEn: "So you mean the test covers chapter one only, right?" This is…

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Record a mini-roleplay (both voices): a friend gives you a complicated instruction; you ask for repetition, ask one meaning, and confirm with a summary.
supportEn: Sorry, could you say that again?

lessonBlocks.noteId: Alat ini menyelamatkan Anda dari arah Ryo yang super singkat di Episode 2 — dan kelak, dari kesalahan besar di tempat magang kelas 12.

questions.promptEn: The strongest way to confirm an instruction is…
optionsEn: repeating the key points in your own words

questions.promptEn: "Could you say that again?" is better than silence because…
questions.explanationId: Inti pelajaran: bertanya = strategi, bukan kelemahan.

questions.promptEn: Choose the polite slow-down request.
optionsEn: "Could you slow down a little, please?"

expressions.expression: Could you slow down a little?

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Explain how to do something, step by step
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Keterampilan penting untuk siswa SMK dan siapa pun yang berbagi cara kerja: menjelaskan prosedur dengan urutan dan peringatan yang jelas.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can explain a simple procedure with clear steps and warnings.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: What is something you know how to do well?
What happens when instructions skip a step?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Prosedur memakai kalimat perintah (imperative), urutan (first, next, then, finally), dan peringatan (make sure, don't, be careful). Untuk alat, sebutkan bahan/alat dulu (You'll need…).

patterns.en: You'll need a memory card and a card reader.
First, plug it in. Then wait for the light to turn green.
Make sure the power is off before you open the case.

lessonBlocks.textEn: HOW TO BORROW A CAMERA FROM THE MEDIA WORKSHOP: You'll need your student card and a booking form. First, check the schedule board to see which cameras are free. Next, fill in the booking form — write the exact times, not "after school". Then bring the form and your student card to the equipment desk. The staff will check the battery and lens with you; don't skip this, or you'll be blamed for old scratches. Finally, sign the log book. Return the camera before 4 p.m. the same day. Be careful with the strap — it looks strong, but it isn't.

tasks.promptEn: What do you need before starting?
tasks.answerEn: A student card and a booking form.

tasks.promptEn: Why must you check the camera with the staff?
tasks.answerEn: So you won't be blamed for existing damage/scratches.

tasks.promptEn: Which step comes right after filling in the form?
tasks.answerEn: Bringing the form and student card to the equipment desk.

tasks.promptEn: Choose the correct warning form.
optionsEn: Make sure the file is saved before closing.
The file makes sure to save.

tasks.promptEn: Turn into a procedure step: "You should press the red button after that."
tasks.answerEn: Then press the red button.

tasks.promptEn: Which marker introduces the last step?

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Explain a procedure you actually know (charging a phone safely, making instant noodles properly, setting up a tripod) in 5 steps with one "make sure" warning. Record 45 seconds.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Anda membantu Ryo mendemonstrasikan prosedur bengkel media kepada Maya di Episode 8.

questions.promptEn: "You'll need…" at the start of a procedure introduces…
"Don't skip this, or you'll be blamed" — "or" here means…
optionsEn: the consequence if you ignore the step

questions.promptEn: Why write "the exact times, not 'after school'"?
questions.explanationId: Prosedur yang baik menghilangkan tafsir ganda.

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Write short messages that get answered
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menulis pesan singkat yang jelas dan sesuai konteks: kepada teman, dan versi lebih sopan kepada kakak kelas atau guru.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can write clear short messages with the right level of politeness.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: How is texting a friend different from texting a teacher?
What makes a message confusing?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Pesan singkat yang baik menjawab: siapa Anda (jika perlu), apa yang Anda butuhkan, dan kapan. Kepada teman boleh sangat santai; kepada guru/kakak kelas tambahkan sapaan, "please", dan terima kasih. Jangan kirim "hello" saja lalu diam.

patterns.en: Hey, running 10 mins late. Order me an es teh?
Good afternoon, Sir. This is Dinda from 10-B. May I ask about tomorrow's assignment?
patterns.noteId: Pesan ke guru: sapaan + identitas + permintaan sopan.

patterns.en: Got it, thanks!

lessonBlocks.textEn: CHAT 1 — To a friend: "gate's locked?? where r u" / "side entrance, near the workshop. hurry, hana's counting people" / "omw 2 min" — CHAT 2 — To a senior: "Good afternoon, Kak Nadia. This is the new member from 10-B. Hana asked me to confirm: is the meeting still at 3 in Club Room 2? Thank you!" / "Hi! Yes, still at 3. Bring your form if you haven't handed it in." / "Will do. See you there!"

tasks.promptEn: Why is Chat 1 acceptable even with short forms?
tasks.answerEn: Between friends, speed and clarity matter; both understand the context.

tasks.promptEn: What three things does the Chat 2 opener include?
tasks.answerEn: Greeting, identity, and a clear question.
tasks.explanationId: Kepada orang yang lebih senior, konteks wajib.

tasks.promptEn: What does "omw" mean?
tasks.answerEn: On my way.

tasks.promptEn: Which message to a teacher is appropriate?
optionsEn: "Good morning, Bu. This is Raka from 10-A. May I confirm the homework deadline? Thank you."

tasks.promptEn: Shorten for a close friend: "I am currently on my way and will arrive in approximately five minutes."
tasks.answerEn: omw, 5 min

tasks.promptEn: You need a reply today. Which message works best?
optionsEn: "Hi Kak, quick question — can I still hand in the form today? Need to know before 4."
tasks.explanationId: Kebutuhan + tenggat membuat pesan mudah dijawab.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Write two versions of the same request (borrowing a book you left in class): one chat to a close friend, one message to your homeroom teacher.
supportEn: Teacher: greeting + identity + polite request + thanks.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Anda akan membalas email Maya dan mengoordinasikan kunjungan lewat pesan di Episode 7.

questions.promptEn: Sending only "hello" to a busy person is weak because…
optionsEn: it gives them nothing to answer

questions.promptEn: Which element does a message to a senior need that a friend-chat doesn't?
"Will do" as a reply means…

lessonBlocks.titleEn: Tell a story people actually listen to
lessonBlocks.instructionId: Anda akan menceritakan kisah sederhana secara lisan: pembukaan yang menarik, urutan jelas, puncak, dan penutup yang bermakna.
lessonBlocks.canDoEn: I can tell a short connected story with a hook, a build-up, and an ending.

lessonBlocks.instructionId: Jawab singkat.
promptsEn: Who is the best storyteller you know? What makes them good?
What is the difference between a report and a story?

lessonBlocks.explanationId: Cerita lisan hidup karena: pembuka yang memancing ("You won't believe what happened…"), penanda urutan (at first, then, suddenly), penahan perhatian ("and guess what?"), dan penutup yang memberi makna. Gunakan suara dan jeda, bukan hanya kata.

patterns.en: Okay, so this happened last week…
At first everything was fine. Then, suddenly…
And that's how I learned to always check the schedule.

lessonBlocks.transcriptEn: Okay, so this happened on my second day here. I wanted to look confident, right? So I walked into class like I owned the place, sat down, took out my books. At first, everything was fine. Then the teacher walked in — and guess what? It wasn't my class. Not even my grade. Twenty seniors staring at me. I just… stood up, bowed a little for some reason, and walked out in total silence. And that's how the whole school learned my name in one day. Honestly? Best accident ever.

tasks.promptEn: What is the story's hook?
tasks.answerEn: "Okay, so this happened on my second day here" — plus wanting to look confident.

tasks.promptEn: Which phrase builds the surprise?
tasks.answerEn: "Then the teacher walked in — and guess what?"

tasks.promptEn: How does the ending give the story meaning?
tasks.answerEn: It reframes the embarrassment as "best accident ever" — everyone learned the speaker's name.
tasks.explanationId: Penutup reflektif mengubah kejadian jadi cerita.

lessonBlocks.audioDisclosureId: Suara ini dibuat oleh AI dan menunggu persetujuan final editor akademik.

tasks.promptEn: Which opener makes people want to listen?
optionsEn: "You won't believe what happened at practice yesterday."

tasks.promptEn: Add tension: "Everything was fine. The lights went off."
tasks.answerEn: At first everything was fine. Then, suddenly, the lights went off.

tasks.promptEn: A good story ending usually…
optionsEn: connects the event to a feeling or lesson
tasks.explanationId: Penutup memberi alasan cerita itu layak didengar.

lessonBlocks.promptEn: Tell a 60-second true story from your first weeks at school: hook, build-up with "at first/then/suddenly", one "guess what" moment, and a closing line about what it meant.

lessonBlocks.noteId: Di Episode 9 Anda menceritakan kisah semester ini di depan klub — dan di kelulusan kelak, kisah tiga tahun.

questions.promptEn: "And guess what?" in a story is used to…
"At first… then suddenly…" creates…
What separates a story from a report?
optionsEn: Stories have feelings, tension, and a point; reports list facts.
